message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): POST 3xx no-follow verify-parity (tick 385)

Added `distributed_http_client_post_returns_3xx_redirect_as_ok_response`
— POST-verb variant of the ft-kfkyi 3xx no-follow contract test (tick
351, GET).

Why: ft-kfkyi's fix in tick 351 configured RedirectPolicy at the
client CONSTRUCTION site (`.no_redirects()` on the asupersync
HttpClient builder). Both GET and POST route through the same inner
client, so in principle they inherit the same policy. This test
verifies empirically that POST also refuses to follow a 302 —
doesn't just assume the builder-level policy propagates.

Regression this catches: if a future refactor wrapped GET but not
POST with a per-verb redirect override (or vice versa), only the
unwrapped verb would transparently follow redirects. The existing
GET-only test wouldn't catch it.

POST 3xx coverage now matches the layered coverage for GET:
  - GET   Ok(302) for unresolvable Location — tick 351
  - GET   no-follow with resolvable Location — tick 352
  - POST  Ok(302) for unresolvable Location — this tick
  (A POST companion with resolvable-Location could be added later,
  but GET's companion already pins the cross-site no-follow
  contract at the client layer.)

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 13s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
